The Hong Kong government will set up a collaborative mechanism with the legislature to craft the city’s first five-year plan to align with national development, according to Chief Executive John Lee Ka-chiu, who said the blueprint will be ready by the end of this year.

news.headline: As American Bombers Take Off From R.A.F. Fairford in UK, Iraq War Looms Large
news.summary: R.A.F. Fairford was the site of repeated antiwar protests during the Iraq war in 2003. Now it is being used again as a base for U.S. bombing missions in the Middle East.

news.headline: China and Chile wrap up Atacama Trench mission as US pressure mounts
news.summary:
Chinese and Chilean scientists have reached the bottom of the Atacama Trench, where light does not penetrate, in what they called the “most ambitious” deep-sea expedition ever conducted in the eastern Pacific.

news.headline: Cuba faces nationwide blackouts amid US oil blockade
news.summary: Cuba’s national power grid has collapsed amid an ongoing US-imposed oil blockade, causing blackouts across the country.

news.headline: Japan to raise residency fees by up to 2,900% despite deepening labour crisis
news.summary:
Settling in Japan as a foreign national is about to become much more expensive after the government passed legislation to sharply increase visa application fees.

news.headline: Millions without electricity as Cuba's power grid collapses
news.summary: Cuba's chronic fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US blockade on oil shipments to the island.
